summary.intent: Conduct a comprehensive 5-domain parallel code review of the optimize-anything project using specialized agents, then autonomously fix all identified issues and ship them via a ralph-loop.
summary.outcome: All 4 batches of fixes were applied and pushed to main: 10 critical issues resolved, 258 tests passing (up from 252), LICENSE added, 6 missing command stubs created, and documentation corrected.

repo: plugin.json declared 7 slash commands but only commands/optimize.md existed; Claude Code discovers commands via filesystem .md files, not the manifest array
MIT license was declared in pyproject.toml but no LICENSE file existed at repo root — a hard marketplace blocker
CLAUDE.md is the most accurate reference doc; AGENTS.md was the most stale (missing analyze subcommand and 2 modules)
evaluator-cookbook.md documented only 2 of 3 evaluator types — LLM judge was entirely absent
args.budget == 100 in _apply_spec_to_args was used as a sentinel to detect CLI default vs explicit user value — cannot distinguish them
All scripts invoke CLI via subprocess (not direct imports), so they test the real entry point and catch integration issues unit tests miss
DEFAULT_QUALITY_DIMENSIONS is shared between intake.py and evaluator_generator.py — correct single-source-of-truth pattern
test_doc_contract.py uses regex to guard against documentation drift — an underappreciated pattern in this codebase

code.finding: Budget sentinel bug: `if args.budget == 100` cannot distinguish user-explicit --budget 100 from argparse default; spec file silently wins. Fix: change argparse default to None, apply 100 fallback after spec merging.
subprocess.run called without timeout= argument; CI gate hangs forever if evaluator stalls. Every other subprocess call in the codebase passes an explicit timeout.
llm_judge_evaluator swallows exceptions into (0.0, {error:...}) tuples while analyze_for_dimensions at lines 360-361 raises RuntimeError — incompatible error contracts in the same module.
validate_evaluator_payload (preflight) and _parse_evaluator_result (runtime) duplicate the same validation logic and can silently diverge on future contract changes.
_normalize_quality_dimensions collects names into a list without checking for duplicates; _compute_weighted_score would double-count a repeated dimension name.
except (ValueError, Exception) is redundant — Exception is the base class of ValueError, swallowing programming errors. Should be except ValueError or except json.JSONDecodeError.
--evaluator-command is extended into cmd before other flags, contradicting the documented nargs='+' greedy-parsing contract that requires it to be last.
Code fence stripping logic inlined in _parse_judge_response is identical to _strip_code_fences defined at lines 290-298; the inline version should call the helper.
_extract_failure_metric_signal uses string-token matching (failure_tokens tuple) as a heuristic — a metric named 'detail_of_failure_modes' would spuriously trigger it.
Magic string '__optimize_anything_preflight__' appears in two locations without a shared constant — it's a protocol constant evaluator scripts may special-case.

workflow: Domain-specialized parallel agents (5 agents by expertise domain) outperform file-by-file agents: each reads only its slice (~1.5-4K lines vs 8K total), runs in parallel, and catches cross-cutting patterns within its domain
Ralph loop with machine-verifiable gates (pytest, file existence checks, grep) is effective for autonomous iterative fixing — encode completion criteria as shell commands with expected outputs
Ralph loop skill had a bug (issue #16398): ```! code block syntax caused shell to interpret <promise> tags in arguments; fix was to replace the block with Bash() function call in the command .md file
Ralph loop plugin fixes apply to the local cache at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/ — they are ephemeral and will be overwritten on plugin update
Structured review reports with Critical/Moderate/Minor tiers and confidence scores allow efficient deduplication when merging multiple agent outputs into a unified action plan

friction: Ralph loop skill was broken before use — required reading GitHub issue and manually patching the cached plugin file before the loop could launch
Two consecutive API 500 errors interrupted the session during the ralph-loop setup phase, requiring the user to resubmit the same command twice
The plugin cache fix is local and ephemeral — the next claude plugin update will overwrite the patched ralph-loop.md and scripts
open_items: consecutive_smoke_gate.py is entirely untested — no tests for _run_pass, parse_args, main, or --budget bounds validation (1-3)
~60 lines of evaluator-selection logic is still duplicated verbatim between _cmd_optimize and _cmd_score in cli.py — a shared _build_evaluator() factory was identified but not implemented
_load_and_normalize_intake_spec + intake_requested sentinel pattern still repeated 3x in CLI command handlers
evaluator generator templates (_generate_command_evaluator and _generate_http_evaluator) still copy-paste the full dimension scoring logic
_load_spec_if_provided still returns dict | None | False (three-state) — non-idiomatic error signaling
Score range 0-1 is documented as a convention in CONCEPTS.md but not enforced in code; validate_evaluator_payload only checks numeric/finite
README missing --temperature and --timeout flag documentation for the analyze subcommand
WALKTHROUGH.md doesn't include analyze as a step between the RED and GREEN phases where it naturally fits
Skills directories lack examples/ subdirectories with sample evaluator scripts and optimization run outputs
score_check.py hardcodes ['bash', evaluator_path] — constrains baseline regression suite to bash-only evaluators
